message:
fix: unblock navigation away from Dashboard and My Clusters (#7833)

* fix: unblock navigation away from Dashboard and My Clusters

Navigating away from the main Dashboard or My Clusters pages required
two clicks — the URL changed but the destination page didn't render
until well after the transition should have committed. Navigating away
from Deploy (16 cards) was instant, even though Dashboard has only 10
cards and My Clusters only 7. So it wasn't render work, it was the
scheduler.

Root cause: both Dashboard and My Clusters are heavy consumers of
useClusters(), which subscribes to the shared clusterCache. Every time
updateClusterCache() fires — on every poll tick, on every per-cluster
health-check completion (checkHealthProgressively fans these out in
parallel), and on every WebSocket change notification — the subscriber
calls setLocalState(cache) synchronously on every component using the
hook. React treats those as urgent state updates, and the router's
internal startTransition gets starved: the new route can't commit
because the tree is always busy re-rendering cluster subscribers.

Deploy uses useDeployments (different cache, no per-item health
fan-out), which is why it wasn't affected.

Fix: wrap the subscriber's setLocalState in startTransition so cluster
cache updates become interruptible. Navigation stays urgent; cluster
poll/health-check updates become low-priority and yield to it. The
initial sync at subscription time is kept urgent so the component
hydrates with correct data on mount — only subsequent cache
notifications are transitioned.

Also remove the reloadDocument attribute from the sidebar NavLink.
It was added in a previous attempt to force a hard navigation as a
workaround, but it causes the page to blink on every click, which
is worse than the problem it was meant to solve. With the scheduler
unblocked, SPA navigation works without it.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: wrap cluster subscriber notify at source, not per-consumer

Initial attempt wrapped only useClusters()'s own setLocalState in
startTransition, but cluster cache notifications fan out to many
other subscribers across the app (every card that reads cluster data).
Those still fired as urgent setStates and kept starving the router's
transition from Dashboard and My Clusters.

Move the startTransition wrap into notifyClusterSubscribers() itself,
so every subscriber's update — not just useClusters's — becomes
transition-priority. This covers all current and future subscribers
uniformly without each hook having to remember to wrap its own
setState.

Revert the subscriber-side wrap in clusters.ts since the source-side
wrap now covers it.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* revert: cluster cache startTransition wrap caused stuck spinner

The notify-source startTransition wrap deferred all subscriber
updates — including the isRefreshing: true → false transition at
the end of each fetch. Under continuous polling, the "done" update
kept getting superseded by the next fetch's "start" update, so the
refresh indicator (spinner on the logo) never got the chance to
commit back to false. Regression: spinner never stops.

Reverting both the source-side wrap and the per-subscriber wrap.
This leaves the branch with just the sidebar reloadDocument
removal. The navigation slowness from Dashboard and My Clusters
needs a different approach — likely separating UI indicator state
(isRefreshing) from data state (clusters[]) so the data updates
can be transitioned without deferring the indicator.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
